description: Provide robust real-time collaboration for documents via SignalApp, ensuring low-latency presence updates (PresenceTracker, PeerPresence) and consistent document state (DocumentStore, SyncEngine, SyncManager).
Support extensible indexing and search across documents using InvertedIndex, GraphBuilder, and SearchPlugin so users can query documents with SearchQuery and receive SearchResult/SearchHit.
Offer AI-assisted document summarization with pluggable implementations: LocalSummarizer for fast local summaries and RemoteSummarizer for higher-quality remote summaries (Summarizer contract).
Expose a PluginHost/PluginContext model so plugins (ExportPlugin, SearchPlugin) can safely access documents and storage events (StorageEventBus, StorageEvent types) without breaking core invariants.
Ensure operational quality: observability, persistent storage durability, async job processing (background summarization/sync), authentication and adequate test coverage as first-class requirements.
Module boundary signal-app contains core runtime: app/* implements services DocumentStore, SyncEngine, SyncManager, ClockProvider and host-level SignalApp which composes these services.

Contract-driven design: many domain interfaces (Summarizer, DocumentReader, PresenceStatus, InvertedIndex, PluginContext) define behaviors; implementations (LocalSummarizer, RemoteSummarizer, PresenceTracker) are wired in the app module.
Event-driven storage model: StorageEventBus plus typed StorageEventCreated/Updated/Deleted/Linked form the observable boundary for indexing, plugin notifications, and sync propagation.

Sync and conflict model: SyncEngine, SyncManager, VectorClock, ConflictCandidate, and ConflictResolution indicate a vector-clock-based synchronization design with explicit conflict resolution strategies.

Plugin isolation: PluginHost and PluginContext are intended to give plugins controlled access to DocumentStore and runtime services (refactor note: Presence refactor uses PluginContext for document access).

LocalSummarizer is implemented as a pure function (LocalSummarizer.summarize) in app/src/ai/summarizer.ts; RemoteSummarizer is asynchronous and has side effects — two distinct runtime profiles for the Summarizer contract.
PresenceTracker exposes join(peerId, documentId?) returning PeerPresence and has async side effects; leave() is marked pure in the analysis which may mask necessary cleanup I/O.
createValidatorFromStore(_store: DocumentStore) performs I/O and is used in presence validation; this ties presence logic directly to DocumentStore visibility/integrity.

There is an explicit refactor: '[REFACTOR] fix(presence): refactor to use PluginContext for document access and remove dire' — code changed to route document access through PluginContext but the note 'remove dire' suggests a removed primitive that other modules might still depend on.

Multiple service nodes are duplicated in the topology (DocumentStore, SyncEngine, ClockProvider, SyncManager appear more than once) — the runtime likely has multiple instances or name collisions in analysis.

StorageEvent types (Created, Updated, Deleted, Linked) and StorageEventBus exist but there's no explicit Contract: StorageEventBus implementation node surfaced — potential gap between contract and concrete event broker.
Indexing components (InvertedIndex, GraphBuilder, AdjacencyList, IndexStats) are present; GraphBuilder and inverted index roles are separate which implies offline or async indexing stages.

Decision nodes 'TypeScript adoption' and 'Testing adoption' exist — the project contains an explicit decision to adopt TypeScript and increase test coverage, but 'Test coverage required' is flagged as a constraint indicating coverage is not yet sufficient.
Top-level module signal-app contains ~20 source files; overall graph reports 187 nodes and 220 edges implying substantial unexposed or library code beyond the app module.
Authentication required: all remote operations (RemoteSummarizer calls, SyncEngine transport) must validate credentials/authorization before accessing documents or performing sync.

Persistent storage: DocumentStore must persist document versions and version history; SyncEngine/SyncManager rely on durable storage to compute VectorClock, VersionHistory, and ConflictResolution correctly.
Async job processing: background tasks (summarization, indexing, conflict resolution) must be resilient, idempotent, and observable — failures should not cause data loss or inconsistent indices.
Observability: StorageEventBus and SyncManager must emit sufficient events/metrics to reconstruct system state and diagnose sync/conflict incidents (IndexStats, StorageEvent traces).
Input validation boundary and guard clauses: domain invariants (Document, DocumentChange, SearchQuery) must be validated at module boundaries to avoid corrupting InvertedIndex or GraphBuilder state.
RemoteSummarizer network failures or rate limits will cause visible delays in summarization pipelines; without retries and backpressure the summarization queue could backlog and affect SyncManager.

PresenceTracker.join/leave race conditions will surface under concurrent peers: if leave() is implemented as a pure function (no I/O) but must update shared state, presence may leak stale PeerPresence entries.

Index staleness: if StorageEventBus implementation is missing or not hooked into DocumentStore, InvertedIndex and GraphBuilder will be out of sync after DocumentStore updates, causing incorrect SearchResult sets.

After the refactor to use PluginContext for document access, some plugins or consumers still referencing the removed 'dire' primitive will break at runtime causing plugin initialization failures in PluginHost.

ConflictResolution quality will degrade if SyncEngine instances are duplicated or misconfigured (duplicate DocumentStore/SyncEngine nodes observed); redundant instances may create divergent VectorClock histories.

Decision to adopt TypeScript exists but the analysis shows potential discrepancies (duplicate nodes, contracts with weak implementation mapping) indicating partial or uneven adoption across the codebase.

The project intends plugin isolation via PluginContext/PluginHost, yet presence refactor changed document access to PluginContext and removed a primitive — this may contradict existing plugin expectations and break backward compatibility.

Goal of strong observability and complete StorageEvent tracking conflicts with the observation that StorageEventBus implementation is not clearly present, risking blind spots between DocumentStore and indexing/sync layers.

A requirement for test coverage and 'Testing adoption' decision contradicts the number of contracts (71) and entities (51) with complex behaviors (sync, conflict, plugins) — the current test surface likely undercovers critical concurrent and IO-heavy logic.
